title: Argus Raises First Solar Price Target to $275 After Q1 Beat
text: Argus Research upgraded its outlook for First Solar stock on May 13, 2026, increasing the price target by $25 to $275 following the company's strong first-quarter performance. First Solar exceeded expectations with earnings per share of $3.22 versus the anticipated $2.87, alongside revenue of $1.04 billion. Trading near $238.76 on the announcement date, the new target suggests approximately 15% upside from current levels. The photovoltaic solar module manufacturer maintains a conservative balance sheet with a debt-to-equity ratio of 0.02 and solid liquidity metrics (current ratio of 2.56). Despite Argus's optimistic assessment, analyst opinions remain divided. The MarketBeat consensus is a Moderate Buy with an average price target of $247.03. Recent actions show divergence—while Susquehanna lowered its target from $280 to $250 in April, GLJ Research downgraded the stock from buy to hold in March, and DZ Bank assigned a strong sell rating in February. This variance reflects ongoing uncertainty around renewable energy valuations amid shifting policy environments and market conditions.
